scope.selection_note: Codex-only project identity cleanup prep from current data/canonical/projects.jsonl, data/identity-overrides/merges.json, data/identity-overrides/do-not-merge.json, and a read-only reconstruction of validateRepo duplicate_global_identity logic. Proposals were limited to current project duplicate issues where both ids exist, the proposed alias is neither a merge alias nor a current merge target, the target is not a merge alias, the ids differ, no project do-not-merge guard applies, and source/canonical evidence supports one durable project identity.
scope.backlog_caveat: The user described a current 544-issue validation backlog. The read-only duplicate_global_identity reconstruction over current global canonical JSONL observed 547 duplicate_global_identity issues at prep time, including 32 project issues. This note anchors to the current files read during this prep and records the count mismatch as a caveat rather than running validate.

validator_evidence: Global project records project_fordham-rd-bus-priority and project_fordham-rd-bus-priority-cab share identity key project_fordham-rd-bus-priority-project.
proposed_aliases.reason: The validator flags the CAB record and the richer Fordham Road-Inwood record on the same Fordham Road Bus Priority Project identity key. Canonical evidence shows the alias is a one-source January 2021 Fordham Road Bus Priority Project CAB/planning record, while the target is the multi-source Fordham Road-Inwood Bus Priority project that already includes Fordham Road bus-priority study evidence and Major Deegan-to-Boston Road context. Source evidence supports one continuing Fordham Road bus-priority project process rather than a separate child project.

alias.description: Bus priority project on Fordham Road from Major Deegan Expressway to Boston Road, presented to Community Advisory Board in January 2021.

target.description: Study of bus priority treatments along the Bx12 corridor, including Fordham Road and 207th Street/University Heights Bridge connection to Inwood, with three design alternatives under traffic analysis.

source_evidence.quote: Fordham Road Community Advisory Board (CAB) will guide the project before, during, and after implementation
traffic analysis that includes 3 distinct design alternatives for bus priority treatments along the Bx12 corridor

validator_evidence: Global project records project_bx12-sbs-brt-phase2 and project_bx12-sbs-fordham-rd-phase1 share identity key project_fordham-rd-bx12-sbs.
proposed_aliases.reason: The validator flags both records on the Fordham Road Bx12 SBS identity key. Canonical evidence shows both records describe the Fordham Road Bx12 Select Bus Service project, with launched/in-service status and Bronx/Fordham Road scope. Source evidence confirms the alias record is not a separate Phase 2 implementation: the Phase 2 intro source itself says Fordham Road Bx12 SBS service launched in June 2008 and was the first phase of SBS/BRT service; the target source lists Fordham Road Bx12 SBS under the BRT Phase I Program as in service.

alias.description: First Phase I BRT route, launched on Fordham Road in the Bronx in June 2008

target.description: BRT Phase I project. Fordham Road Bx12 Select Bus Service.

source_evidence.quote: Fordham Road Bx12 SBS: service launched June 2008
The City implemented its first phase of BRT service, called Select Bus Service (SBS), on Fordham Road in the Bronx in June of 2008.
BRT Phase I Program A Fordham Road Bx12 SBS \u2013 in service

validator_evidence: Global project records project_hylan-blvd-sbs-brt-phase2 and project_hylan-blvd-sbs-phase1 share identity key project_hylan-blvd-sbs.
proposed_aliases.reason: The validator flags both records on the Hylan Boulevard SBS identity key. Canonical evidence shows both are Hylan Boulevard SBS project records with planned status and Staten Island/Hylan Boulevard SBS scope. Source evidence keeps the project identity aligned: the alias source lists Hylan Boulevard SBS as a Phase I BRT route in planning for a Staten Island south-shore-to-Brooklyn connection, and the target source lists Hylan Boulevard SBS in the BRT Phase I Program with 2013/2014 implementation. This is an overview-source duplication of the same planned SBS project, not a same-name-different-location collision.

alias.description: Phase I BRT route in planning for a faster connection from Staten Island's south shore to Brooklyn

target.description: BRT Phase I project. Hylan Boulevard SBS.

source_evidence.quote: Phase I BRT Routes Route Need Type Status Hylan Boulevard SBS Difficult trip Faster connection from Staten Island's south shore to Brooklyn; in planning
Hylan Boulevard SBS: planning underway for a revised project design
BRT Phase I Program F Hylan Boulevard SBS \u2013 2013/2014 implementation

proposal_guards: Both ids for every proposal were verified as current project record_id values in data/canonical/projects.jsonl.
No proposed alias_id is a current project merge alias in data/identity-overrides/merges.json.
No proposed target_id is a current project merge alias in data/identity-overrides/merges.json.
No proposed alias_id is currently used as a project merge target in data/identity-overrides/merges.json.
Each alias_id differs from its target_id.
No proposed pair hits the current project do-not-merge guard in data/identity-overrides/do-not-merge.json.
blockers_to_requested_8_to_10: Only 32 current project duplicate_global_identity issues were present in the read-only pass, and most remaining project issues failed the requested guards or high-confidence semantic screen.
Excluded project_fordham-rd-bus-priority-cb5-may2026 because the proposed alias is already a current merge target.

Excluded project_bx6-south-bronx-crosstown-sbs, project_faster-cleaner-safer-plan / project_meeting-doc-113946-faster-cleaner-safer, project_b46-sbs-utica-ave-2015-09-10 / project_utica-ave-sbs-2014, project_vn-x4-operational-improvements-safety-fence / project_vn-x4-safety-fence, and project_park-ave-viaduct-phase1 / project_park-avenue-viaduct-repair because the only plausible aliases are already current merge targets.

Excluded the 2017 Street Improvement Project collisions because the current duplicate class mixes Woodhaven Boulevard/Q52-Q53 Queens evidence with M79/79th Street Manhattan evidence; this is a same-name-different-location trap.
Excluded Lexington Avenue 96th-60th / 60th-52nd records as a segment/partial-overlap trap.

Excluded MTA 2020-2024 Capital Program Amendment #2 and #5 collisions because the current target record conflates parent capital-program identity with amendment records; these need a policy decision before adding more aliases.
Excluded ABLE 2022 expansion, 2023 expansion, and ABLE program collisions as likely parent-program versus staged expansion records.
Excluded CBDTP, Congestion Relief, CRZ toll revenue obligation, and TransCore modification collisions as parent-program versus financing/contract-action collisions.

Excluded East Side Access support, Main Line Second Track, Jamaica Capacity Improvement, Hall Interlocking, Penn Reconstruction design, and similar records as support-project, phase, or parent-child collisions.
Excluded LIRR Security Initiatives / NYCT Security Initiatives as a same-name-different-agency trap.

Excluded 34th Street Transitway because the semantically preferable direction would alias the thinner Phase I overview record into the richer concept record, but that overview record is already a current merge target; the reverse direction would choose a weaker target.

command_evidence.purpose: Read validateRepo duplicate_global_identity logic and the deterministic identity helper used to reproduce duplicate-key evidence without running validate.

command_evidence.command: bun --eval '<read-only duplicate-key pass over data/canonical/projects.jsonl using identityKeysForRecord plus current do-not-merge suppression>'
command_evidence.purpose: Reconstructed current project duplicate_global_identity issues without running materialize, validate, export, or provider-backed commands; found 32 project issues.

command_evidence.command: bun --eval '<read-only duplicate-key pass over current entities/projects/corridors/routes canonical JSONL using identityKeysForRecord plus current do-not-merge suppression>'
command_evidence.purpose: Checked the current global duplicate_global_identity backlog caveat; found 547 duplicate_global_identity issues total, by kind: entity 457, project 32, corridor 34, route 24.
Verified proposed aliases are not merge aliases, proposed targets are not merge aliases, and proposed aliases are not current merge targets.

command_evidence.command: jq -r '.pairs.project[]? | select(.record_ids | any(. as $id | [selected proposal ids] | index($id))) | .record_ids | @tsv' data/identity-overrides/do-not-merge.json
command_evidence.purpose: Verified no current project do-not-merge guard covers any proposed pair.

command_evidence.command: bun --eval '<inspect selected project records, payload fields, source_ids, aliases, evidence refs>'
command_evidence.purpose: Read canonical evidence for candidate pairs and screened out phase/stage, same-name-different-location, and parent-program/child-project traps.
Read local source blocks for the evidence quotes cited by the three proposals.
Captured UTC created_at for this prep artifact.
